checklistAnswers.question: Is Household-Related Items approved in the budget?
Is the item related to a need or goal identified in the Life Plan?
Will the item increase independence, or substitute for human assistance?
Does the item increase safety of the participant in their home environment?
Is the item exclusively needed for the participant?
Does the item increase the participant's health and safety?
Does the individual live in their own home? (for Household support only)
Was the same item bought already in this budget year?
Can the item be funded through other sources?

parsed.justification: David has limited mobility and needs a grab bar to transfer safely in the bathroom and reduce fall risk, increasing his independence at home.
parsed.safetyFeatures: Slip-resistant grip; supports up to 500 lbs; ADA-compliant mounting
parsed.valuedOutcome: David will safely and independently manage personal-care routines in his own home.

category.label: Household Related Items (HRI)
capNotes: Capped at $1,500 per budget year; approval valid 3 months.
exclusionList: Direct Clinician services & therapies (OT, PT, Speech, Psychology) and experimental therapies

webChecks.label: The item exists at the linked page, with a visible price
webChecks.criteria: The linked product page must show the requested item with a concrete visible price.

webChecks.label: Item is plausibly NOT on the IDGS/HRI exclusion list
webChecks.criteria: Judge the item against the exclusion list. A laptop/desktop/tablet is 'Computer Hardware' and MUST be flagged Not Met. IMPORTANT: home-safety and accessibility hardware (grab bars, shower seats, ramps, bed rails, non-slip equipment) is the CORE PURPOSE of HRI and is NOT excluded - the 'Health-Related Services/Equipment/Supplies' entry refers to clinical/medical devices, therapies, and medical supplies, not home-safety modifications. When genuinely in doubt, Needs Review.

webChecks.label: Safety features described on the form are supported by the product page
webChecks.criteria: If the form describes safety features, verify the product page supports them. If the form lists no safety features (N/A), mark Needs Review with a note that no safety features were claimed.

webChecks.label: Item price is within the $1,500/budget-year cap
webChecks.criteria: Compare the visible product price against the $1,500 per budget year cap.

findings.note: No website content was captured, so it's not possible to verify if the item exists with a visible price.
findings.label: The item exists at the linked page, with a visible price

findings.note: The requested item, a bathroom safety grab bar, is a home-safety and accessibility hardware item, which is explicitly stated as a core purpose of HRI and not excluded by the 'Health-Related Services/Equipment/Supplies' entry.
findings.label: Item is plausibly NOT on the IDGS/HRI exclusion list

findings.note: The application form did not describe any specific safety features for the item.
findings.label: Safety features described on the form are supported by the product page

findings.note: No website content was captured, so it's not possible to verify the item's price against the $1,500 cap.
findings.label: Item price is within the $1,500/budget-year cap

rateComparison.note: No website content was captured, so the rate could not be found or compared.
